English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Catherine Jane Personius papers
Personius, Catherine Jane, 1904-
Association of State Universities and Land-Grant Colleges
23-14-979
5 cubic feet. 5 cubic feet.
1930-1967,-1940-1967 (bulk). Publications, Research Materials Includes printed and photocopied material, and other papers relating to Personius' work. Collection material in English 2073523
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
July 2010
EAD encoding:
Daniel W. Young, December 2010
Date modified:
RMC Staff, November 2012
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of food and nutrition, Cornell University.
Catherine Personius graduated from Elmira College in 1925 with a degree in home economics and chemistry, subsequently joining the staff as foods instructor. In 1928, she completed her M.A. at Teacher's College, Columbia University and in 1937 her Ph.D. from Cornell, joining the faculty as an assistant professor that same year. From 1940-1943, she was an associate professor at the University of Wisconsin, Madison, and then returned to Cornell as professor and co-chair of the Department of Food and Nutrition and a year later as chair. In 1947, she also became Coordinator of Research for the College and Assistant Director of the Experiment Station. Personius was the first woman to be a faculty representative on the Board of Trustees where she served from 1959-1964. She retired in 1966.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes printed and photocopied material, and other papers relating to Personius' work as a teacher and researcher in home economics, especially relating to her field of food and nutrition and with the Association of State Universities and Land-Grant Colleges.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Biographical Material
Series II. Course Material
Series III. Subject and Reference Files
Series IV. Cornell Committees, Events, Projects, Research
Series V. Outside Associations and Organizations
Series VI. American Home Economics Association (AHEA)
Series VII. Association of State Universities and Land-Grant Colleges (ASULGC)
Series VIII. Northeast Region
Cite As:
Catherine Jane Personius papers, #23-14-979. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Nutritionists. Home economists. Women college teachers. Nutrition. Home economics. Cornell University -- : Faculty.
